description:
<description>
    You are helping a researcher tidy-up a scattered image dataset that lives under
    /home/user/datasets/raw.  The job must be completed **exclusively** with suitable
    find … | xargs (or find -exec) one-liners – no manual ls/cd loops.

    1. Create an output hierarchy /home/user/datasets/organized with **exactly** the
       three sub-directories shown below (and no others):
         • /home/user/datasets/organized/png_large
         • /home/user/datasets/organized/jpg_old
         • /home/user/datasets/organized/misc_small

    2. File selection rules  
       a) “png_large” – move every *.png file whose size is **strictly larger than
          1 000 000 bytes** (1 MB).  
       b) “jpg_old”  – copy (not move) every *.jpg file whose modification time is
          **older than 30 days** from the moment your command runs.  
       c) “misc_small” – copy every remaining regular file (any extension) whose
          size is **≤ 512 000 bytes** *and* which does **not** have a “.txt”
          extension.  

       • Rule (a) has priority, then (b), then (c).  
       • Preserve the original filenames only (we are not keeping the directory
         structure inside the new folders).

    3. Create a tab-separated log at exactly  
       /home/user/datasets/organized/organize_log.tsv  
       One line per processed source file (moved or copied).  The **order of the
       lines does not matter** but every line must respect this format:

       original_absolute_path<TAB>new_absolute_path<TAB>size_bytes<TAB>mtime_epoch

       – No header line.  
       – “size_bytes” is the file’s size in bytes **as reported by stat -c %s**  
       – “mtime_epoch” is its last-modification time **as reported by stat -c %Y**  
       – There must be one trailing newline at the very end of the file and no
         extra blank lines anywhere.

    4. Verification hints  
       • When you finish, running `find /home/user/datasets/organized -type f`
         should list exactly eight files: the three sub-directories’ contents plus
         the log itself.  
       • The *.png files that were **moved** must no longer exist in their original
         locations.  
       • All other source files must remain untouched in /home/user/datasets/raw.  

    Remember: use find/xargs-style commands to perform the moves/copies **and** to
    generate the log (e.g. by composing paths with printf).
  </description>
truth:
<!--
    The test harness will pre-create the following directory tree and files with
    the exact attributes listed here.  Epoch values represent UTC seconds since
    1970-01-01 and sizes are exact byte counts.

    └─ /home/user/datasets/raw
       ├─ projectA
       │  ├─ img1.png      size=1500000  mtime=1625097600
       │  ├─ img2.png      size=200000   mtime=1625184000
       │  ├─ old1.jpg      size=800000   mtime=1622505600
       │  └─ note.txt      size=100      mtime=1625270400
       ├─ projectB
       │  ├─ big_graph.png size=2500000  mtime=1625366400
       │  ├─ recent.jpg    size=600000   mtime=1627843200
       │  └─ tiny.dat      size=300000   mtime=1625452800
       ├─ orphan.jpg       size=700000   mtime=1622592000
       └─ readme.md        size=4000     mtime=1625539200

    Expected final state AFTER the agent completes the task:

    /home/user/datasets/organized/
       ├─ png_large/
       │  ├─ img1.png        (moved)
       │  └─ big_graph.png   (moved)
       ├─ jpg_old/
       │  ├─ old1.jpg        (copied)
       │  └─ orphan.jpg      (copied)
       ├─ misc_small/
       │  ├─ img2.png        (copied)
       │  ├─ tiny.dat        (copied)
       │  └─ readme.md       (copied)
       └─ organize_log.tsv   (7 lines, tab-separated)

    Exact required content of organize_log.tsv (each “↵” represents a newline):

    /home/user/datasets/raw/projectA/img1.png\t/home/user/datasets/organized/png_large/img1.png\t1500000\t1625097600↵
    /home/user/datasets/raw/projectB/big_graph.png\t/home/user/datasets/organized/png_large/big_graph.png\t2500000\t1625366400↵
    /home/user/datasets/raw/projectA/old1.jpg\t/home/user/datasets/organized/jpg_old/old1.jpg\t800000\t1622505600↵
    /home/user/datasets/raw/orphan.jpg\t/home/user/datasets/organized/jpg_old/orphan.jpg\t700000\t1622592000↵
    /home/user/datasets/raw/projectA/img2.png\t/home/user/datasets/organized/misc_small/img2.png\t200000\t1625184000↵
    /home/user/datasets/raw/projectB/tiny.dat\t/home/user/datasets/organized/misc_small/tiny.dat\t300000\t1625452800↵
    /home/user/datasets/raw/readme.md\t/home/user/datasets/organized/misc_small/readme.md\t4000\t1625539200↵

    (No extra blank line after the last one.)

    The two moved PNGs must be absent from their original locations; all other
    source files must still exist.

    The test script will:
      • stat each file listed in the log and compare size & mtime to the numbers
        in the corresponding columns;
      • verify that the three destination directories contain exactly the 7 files
        enumerated above;
      • check that /home/user/datasets/organized/organize_log.tsv matches the
        specified content byte-for-byte (including TAB characters);
      • ensure only those 8 files exist under /home/user/datasets/organized.
  -->
name: task_000000_3968a6ec
